records.ety: [F. partition, L. partitio. See Part, v.]

defs: The act of parting or dividing; the state of being parted; separation; division; distribution; as, the partition of a kingdom.
quotes: And good from bad find no partition.

defs: That which divides or separates; that by which different things, or distinct parts of the same thing, are separated; separating boundary; dividing line or space; specifically, an interior wall dividing one part or apartment of a house, a compartment of a room, an inclosure, or the like, from another; as, a brick partition; lath and plaster partitions; cubicles with four-foot high partitions.
quotes: No sight could pass Betwixt the nice partitions of the grass.

defs: A part divided off by walls; an apartment; a compartment.
The severance of common or undivided interests, particularly in real estate. It may be effected by consent of parties, or by compulsion of law.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Partitioned (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Partitioning.]

defs: To divide into parts or shares; to divide and distribute; as, to partition an estate among various heirs.
To divide into distinct parts by lines, walls, etc.; as, to partition a house.
quotes: Uniform without, though severally partitioned within.
